source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: As our product designer, you will own the end-to-end design of our developer-facing product, with an eye toward making powerful technology accessible far beyond a technical audience.. That means reimagining the playground, bringing coherence to our API architecture and navigation, and building the systems that scale across surfaces. You’ll shape what we build alongside engineering and product from the start, not after scoping is done.

You: You’ve shipped developer-facing product and you know what makes or breaks a developer’s first hour with a new tool. Your portfolio shows high visual craft without losing function, and end-to-end ownership from exploration to production. You think in systems, push back when the reasoning is sound, and move fast without being precious. You’ll be opening PRs here, so frontend comfort is a real advantage, but what matters more is that you don’t treat the line between design and engineering as someone else’s problem.




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.



We seek like-minded individuals who share our passion for applying science, creativity, and consistency to big and complex problems with equally big outcomes. These are our values:

 - Own customer impact: It’s on us to ensure real-world outcomes for our customers.

 - Obsess over craft: Perfect every detail because quality compounds.

 - Accelerate change: Ship fast, adapt faster, and move frontier ideas into production.

 - Create win-wins: Creatively turn trade-offs into upside.

 - Make high-conviction bets: Try and fail. But succeed an unfair amount.




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Competitive salary

 - Generous equity

 - Visa sponsorships

 - 401K plans

 - Daily lunch & office snacks

 - Dinner at the office

 - Unlimited vacation

 - Caltrain pass reimbursement

source_jobs.title: Early Career Research Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.




ABOUT YOU

You're a researcher who thinks like an engineer, or an engineer who thinks like a researcher. You've worked on information retrieval systems, embedding models, or neural ranking at scale, or you're deeply curious about the fundamental problems that emerge when training models to understand and serve billions of web documents. You thrive in the space between theory and production, where elegant solutions must also run efficiently on real infrastructure. You're comfortable reading papers from SIGIR and RecSys one day and debugging distributed training pipelines the next.




THE ROLE

You'll design and train the models that power Parallel's APIs: the intelligence layer that helps AI agents find exactly what they need from the open web. This means tackling research problems that most labs encounter only at hyperscale: How do you train embedding models that capture semantic intent across diverse query types? How do you balance model expressiveness with sub-second retrieval latency? How do you maintain index freshness when the web updates constantly, without rebuilding from scratch?



Unlike traditional search engines built for human queries, you're building for AI agents that issue complex, multi-hop queries and expect structured, programmatic responses. This is information retrieval reimagined for the LLM era, work that combines classical IR techniques with modern deep learning, applied at a scale that demands new solutions.




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.



We seek like-minded individuals who share our passion for applying science, creativity, and consistency to big and complex problems with equally big outcomes. These are our values:

 - Own customer impact: It’s on us to ensure real-world outcomes for our customers.

 - Obsess over craft: Perfect every detail because quality compounds.

 - Accelerate change: Ship fast, adapt faster, and move frontier ideas into production.

 - Create win-wins: Creatively turn trade-offs into upside.

 - Make high-conviction bets: Try and fail. But succeed an unfair amount.




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Competitive salary

 - Generous equity

 - Visa sponsorships

 - 401K plans

 - Daily lunch & office snacks

 - Dinner at the office

 - Unlimited vacation

 - Caltrain pass reimbursement

source_jobs.title: People Ops
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: You will be Parallel's first People Ops hire, which means you own everything from the employee lifecycle to the compliance infrastructure that lets us scale past the thresholds that matter. Rippling is already in place — your job isn't to implement systems, it's to run them well and build the layer of process and judgment on top that a growing company actually needs. You'll manage benefits administration, onboarding and offboarding, employee relations, and compliance.

You: You've spent five or more years in People Ops, at least some of it at a startup where you were building things that didn't exist yet. You know employment law well enough to spot a compliance gap before it becomes a problem, and you've managed HRIS platforms — Rippling or something close — without treating them as an excuse not to think. You've handled sensitive employee matters with discretion and come out with trust intact. You're not waiting for a playbook and you don't need one handed to you — you write it.




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff, Model Training
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: You will own the training pipeline behind the models that power both Parallel’s search stack and Parallel’s agents. On the search side, that means the rankers, classifiers, and query models that surface the right information. On the agent side, that means the models that help our agents plan, reason, and execute high-value tasks over web data. You will build the path from real product usage to high-quality training data, fine-tune and evaluate these models rigorously, and ship them safely to traffic used by millions.

You: Have deep intuition on modern models and training, including transformer fine-tuning, data curation, and the craft of label quality. Think rigorously about how ranking, retrieval, and agent behavior inform one another, and how to train models that serve all three. You care about your research being applied to product and systems that millions use.




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff, Front-end
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.




ABOUT YOU

You're an expert at modern front-end frameworks, maintainable UI architecture, and building UIs that feel intuitive, fast, and beautiful. You have strong production experience with TypeScript, modern CSS, Vercel, and Sanity (or similar headless CMS platforms).



You understand responsive design, web performance, and accessibility. You can read API documentation, work effectively with backend teams, and have good instincts for what makes a developer tool feel intuitive versus frustrating. Experience with data visualization or technical documentation sites is a plus.




THE ROLE

Parallel is growing rapidly and in need of an experienced front end development lead—someone to take our web properties from their early iterations to a higher bar of excellence as we expand to serve more customers.



You'll design, build, and refine the web experiences that showcase what our powerful APIs are capable of. You'll focus on three key properties:



 - Parallel.ai http://Parallel.ai — our marketing website

 - Platform.parallel.ai http://Platform.parallel.ai — our developer playground and dashboard

 - Docs.parallel.ai http://Docs.parallel.ai — our technical documentation




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead web deployment and hosting operations across our web properties

 - Work with product and marketing teams to develop compelling web experiences that showcase Parallel's core messaging and product features

 - Collaborate with dev rel and forward-deployed engineers on demos and cookbooks that help developers understand technical concepts like structured web search, confidence scoring, and multi-hop agentic research

 - Own the technical foundation and architecture decisions for our web presence as we scale




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.

source_jobs.title: Marketing Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: You will run Parallel's day-to-day marketing execution. You'll work with customers to produce written and video testimonials, turning happy users into case studies that sales can put in front of prospects. You'll set up and keep a regular newsletter cadence covering launch recaps and other marketing collateral, and publish the monthly "what we launched" roundup via Slackbot. You'll coordinate event and hackathon logistics with partners — venues, schedules, promotion, follow-up — and post launch content to the PWS subreddit and other developer communities where our users live. You'll work closely with the founding team and product marketing, and you'll ship something most weeks.



You: You've spent a year or two in marketing, ideally somewhere small, and you got real things out the door — a newsletter people opened, an event that ran on time, a customer story that got used. You write clearly and quickly, and you're comfortable on Reddit: you know how developer communities talk and what earns upvotes versus eye-rolls. You're organized enough to juggle a video shoot, an event checklist, and a launch post in the same week without dropping any of them. You don't wait for a detailed brief — you ask good questions, take a first pass, and improve it fast. You want to grow by owning more, not by watching.




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.

source_jobs.title: Field Marketing
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: You will own Parallel's presence in the field. You'll build and run the event engine — conferences, hackathons, meetups, executive dinners, and partner co-marketing events — that puts Parallel in front of AI engineers and technical buyers. Which events we invest in, how we show up, and what pipeline comes out the other side are your decisions. You'll own the budget, the target lists, and the logistics end to end, with no coordinator behind you. You'll build target lists with the sales team before every event and run the follow-up motion after, so no lead dies in a spreadsheet. You'll be measured in meetings and pipeline, not badge scans.



You: You've run a multi-format event portfolio at a company selling to technical audiences, and you talk about it in pipeline, not attendance. You know which events AI engineers actually go to and that a hackathon earns more trust than a booth. You've hosted a dinner where the programming — not the venue — made the room worth everyone's evening. You've negotiated sponsorships hard, walked away from popular conferences your buyers weren't attending, and killed formats that didn't convert. You run flawless logistics solo, stay calm when something breaks day-of, and treat the sales handoff as your job, not someone else's. You'd rather run three events that produce pipeline than ten that produce photos.




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.

source_jobs.title: Developer Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: You will make Parallel the default search layer across the AI developer ecosystem. You'll own a pipeline spanning agent frameworks, coding tools, inference platforms, workflow builders, routing layers, and open source chat interfaces. The work includes getting native integrations shipped into major OSS projects, building commercial partnerships with platform companies, and managing ongoing relationships post-launch. You'll also own the marketing side of these partnerships: co-authored blog posts and launch announcements, joint content with partner developer relations teams, and technical guides that show developers how to use Parallel within each platform. You'll track competitive positioning across the pipeline, drive technical co-builds with engineering, and move deals from first outreach to shipped integration to public launch story. You'll also keep the pipeline growing by identifying new tools and platforms as they emerge and prioritizing based on distribution potential.

You: Understand the AI developer tools landscape and can speak credibly about agent frameworks, MCP, inference APIs, and how search fits into these workflows. You've done some combination of developer ecosystem partnerships, open source BD, or platform integrations at an infrastructure company. You're comfortable navigating GitHub PRs, reading API docs, and scoping what an integration requires technically, even if you're not writing the code. You can run 30+ concurrent partner conversations without dropping threads, and you know how to prioritize ruthlessly when everything looks like a P0.




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.

We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: Join our deployed engineering team - you will work closest with our customers, help them get the most out of our API, and scale with them. You will understand their product and use-cases deeply, and understand our API. You will be the power user of our API.

You: Love working hands-on with customers. You are an engineer by training but also may have worked in consulting/PM/GTM or roles that are close to customers. You care most about owning customer impact.




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.



We seek like-minded individuals who share our passion for applying science, creativity, and consistency to big and complex problems with equally big outcomes. These are our values:

 - Own customer impact: It’s on us to ensure real-world outcomes for our customers.

 - Obsess over craft: Perfect every detail because quality compounds.

 - Accelerate change: Ship fast, adapt faster, and move frontier ideas into production.

 - Create win-wins: Creatively turn trade-offs into upside.

 - Make high-conviction bets: Try and fail. But succeed an unfair amount.




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Competitive salary

 - Generous equity

 - Visa sponsorships

 - 401K plans

 - Daily lunch & office snacks

 - Dinner at the office

 - Unlimited vacation

 - Caltrain pass reimbursement

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff, Search Ranking
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: You will own the multi-stage ranking pipeline that narrows a web-scale set of candidates down to the handful of best passages for a query, spending the least compute necessary to do it well. You will push precision and recall across fast candidate retrieval, lightweight first-pass ranking, and heavier neural reranking, decide how much compute each query deserves, and grade everything on real usage outcomes rather than offline proxies.

You: Have deep intuition for relevance, feature engineering, and the trade-offs between quality, latency, and cost. Think rigorously about how ranking, retrieval, and agent behavior inform one another, and how to train models that serve all three. You care about your research being applied to product and systems that millions use.




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.



We seek like-minded individuals who share our passion for applying science, creativity, and consistency to big and complex problems with equally big outcomes. These are our values:

 - Own customer impact: It’s on us to ensure real-world outcomes for our customers.

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: Parallel is growing quickly and we're making our first GTM Finance hire to support that growth. You'll work directly with GTM leadership on hiring plans, quota setting, budgeting, and spend. You'll track the metrics that matter—pipeline coverage, quota attainment, commissions, funnel health—and turn them into clear recommendations. Your work will inform pricing and packaging from a financial and analytical lens. Ultimately, you’ll get a front row seat to a fast-growing AI-native startup, with hands-on experience across finance and operations.

You: Analytical but practical. You've worked in a high-rigor analytical environment (consulting, banking, PE, etc.) and you've also spent meaningful time at a startup, ideally in B2B software or AI. You know GTM metrics well and have worked closely with sales or GTM teams before on topics like pricing and deal desk. You're comfortable with AI tools and have used them to do real work. You have somewhere between 4 and 8 years of experience total.

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff, Retrieval & Indexing
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: You will build and operate the systems that let Parallel run its own large-scale full-text index over the web. You will work across the full lifecycle: deciding what belongs in the corpus, building the searchable structures that hold it, building the signals and models that judge source quality and freshness, and serving queries against the index quickly and economically. You will anticipate bottlenecks before they appear and ensure the architecture evolves to meet increasing demand.

You: Have deep intuition on information retrieval, distributed systems, and performance tuning. You like to reason about the trade-offs between classical and neural retrieval, and between cost, reliability, and latency. You care about your work helping agents find the right information, and about infrastructure that can support products used by millions without breaking a sweat.




LIFE AT PARALLEL

Our team works fully in-person, between our Palo Alto HQ and San Francisco office. We’re a flat, talent-dense organization dedicated to solving technical and creative problems.

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Parallel is a web infrastructure company. Our products are used by leading businesses in sales, marketing, insurance, and coding to build best-in-class AI agents with flexible and powerful programmatic access to the web.



We've raised $230 million from Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, Index Ventures, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures, First Round, and Terrain to build the web for AIs. We're currently valued at $2 billion and we're forming a world-class team of engineers, designers, marketers, sellers, researchers, and operational experts to achieve our mission.



Job: You will own Parallel's relationships with cloud providers. You'll build and run co-sell motions with cloud sales teams, manage marketplace listings and transactions, and develop joint go-to-market programs that drive enterprise pipeline. You'll work closely with cloud partner managers to get Parallel positioned in their AI and data narratives, and coordinate internally across sales, engineering, and marketing to make sure we're getting full value from each partnership.

You: Have run cloud partnerships at an infrastructure or platform company and know how these organizations work. You've built and navigated partner programs, managed marketplace listings, and built co-sell relationships with cloud field teams. You understand how cloud sales cycles move, how to get your product into a cloud rep's pitch, and how to structure deals that work for both sides. You can manage complex multi-stakeholder relationships across different cloud organizations simultaneously without losing momentum
